quiz.title: Unit 4 Quiz 7, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Lewis Structures for Ionic Compounds
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Lewis Dot Structures for Ionic Compounds, Representing Ions, Electron Transfer, Octet Rule in Ions, Charge Indication

questions.question: In a Lewis structure for NaCl, sodium is shown as:
questions.explanation: In ionic Lewis structures, the cation (Na⁺) is shown with no dots because it has lost its valence electron. The charge is indicated as a superscript, and no dots are shown around it.

questions.question: In ionic Lewis structures, dots represent:
questions.explanation: In Lewis structures, dots represent valence electrons. For ions, the number of dots indicates how many valence electrons remain after gaining or losing electrons.

questions.question: Which particle is shown with a full octet in ionic Lewis structures?
questions.explanation: Non-metal ions (anions) are shown with a full octet (8 valence electrons) in ionic Lewis structures because they gain electrons to achieve a stable noble gas configuration.

questions.question: In the Lewis structure of MgCl₂, magnesium is represented as:
questions.explanation: Magnesium in MgCl₂ is represented as Mg²⁺ with no dots because it has lost both valence electrons. The 2+ charge is shown, and no valence electrons remain.

questions.question: Each chloride ion (Cl⁻) in a Lewis structure has:
questions.explanation: Each Cl⁻ ion has 8 valence electrons (a full octet) in its Lewis structure. Chlorine gains one electron to achieve the electron configuration of argon.

questions.question: Which compound's Lewis structure shows two ions with +1 and -1 charges?
questions.explanation: KCl (potassium chloride) shows K⁺ and Cl⁻ ions — one with +1 charge and one with -1 charge. MgO has +2 and -2, CaCl₂ has one Ca²⁺ and two Cl⁻, and Al₂O₃ has two Al³⁺ and three O²⁻.

questions.question: In CaO, calcium forms which ion?
questions.explanation: Calcium (Group 2) forms a Ca²⁺ ion by losing its two valence electrons. This gives it a stable electron configuration like argon.

questions.question: In the Lewis structure of CaO, oxygen is shown as:
questions.explanation: Oxygen in CaO is shown as O²⁻ with 8 dots (a full octet). It gains two electrons from calcium to achieve the electron configuration of neon.

questions.question: In the Lewis structure of AlCl₃, aluminum is shown as:
options: Al with no dots and no charge
questions.explanation: In ionic representation, aluminum (Group 13) loses its 3 valence electrons to form Al³⁺, shown with no dots and a 3+ charge. Each chloride ion (Cl⁻) is shown separately with 8 dots and a 1- charge.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly represents the Lewis structure for MgO?
options: Mg²⁺ with no dots and O²⁻ with 8 dots
Mg with 2 dots and O with 6 dots sharing electrons
Mg⁺ with 1 dot and O⁻ with 7 dots
Mg²⁺ with 8 dots and O²⁻ with no dots
questions.explanation: In the ionic compound MgO, magnesium loses its two valence electrons to form Mg²⁺, which is shown with no dots and a 2+ charge. Oxygen gains those two electrons to form O²⁻, which is shown with 8 dots (a full octet) and a 2− charge. The structure shows ion charges and complete electron transfer, not shared electrons.

questions.question: What is the correct Lewis structure for potassium sulfide (K₂S)?
options: Two K⁺ ions each with no dots and one S²⁻ ion with 8 dots
Two K atoms each with 1 dot sharing electrons with one S atom
One K₂⁺ ion with 2 dots and one S²⁻ ion with 6 dots
Two K⁻ ions each with 8 dots and one S²⁺ ion with no dots
questions.explanation: In K₂S, each potassium atom (Group 1) loses its one valence electron to form K⁺, shown with no dots and a +1 charge. Sulfur (Group 16) gains two electrons (one from each potassium) to form S²⁻, which is shown with 8 dots (a full octet) and a 2− charge. The Lewis structure shows two separate K⁺ ions and one S²⁻ ion, indicating complete electron transfer.

questions.question: In the Lewis structure of Na₂S, sulfur is shown with:
questions.explanation: Sulfur in Na₂S gains two electrons to form S²⁻, giving it a full octet of 8 valence electrons and a 2- charge.

questions.question: In ionic Lewis structures, metals achieve stability by:
questions.explanation: Metals achieve stability by losing electrons to form cations. This gives them a stable electron configuration, often like the preceding noble gas.

questions.question: In CaCl₂, how many dots surround each chloride ion?
questions.explanation: Each chloride ion (Cl⁻) in CaCl₂ has 8 dots surrounding it, representing a full octet of valence electrons after gaining one electron from calcium.

questions.question: The main purpose of Lewis structures for ionic compounds is to show:
questions.explanation: Lewis structures for ionic compounds primarily show ion charges and electron transfer. They indicate which atoms have lost electrons (cations) and which have gained electrons (anions), along with their resulting charges. Molecular shape and bond angles are shown in covalent Lewis structures.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered Lewis structures for ionic compounds! ⚛️🔌
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review how charges are represented and electron counts for ions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on which elements form cations vs anions and their resulting electron configurations.
scoreMessages.0: Please review ionic Lewis structures, including how to show charges and valence electrons for ions.
